Name: Pepper & Paper Apartments
Notes: Arrival night (Jul 19). 1 night, EUR 215.35

Name: Ukio Tular 1979
Notes: Main stay Jul 20 - Aug 21 (32 nights). EUR 5,555. Bir-Hakeim / Dupleix metro.

Name: Bastide du Mourre
Notes: Aug 21-24 (3 nights). Hyatt points. Conf# 42732343

Name: Hotel Elysee Montmartre
Notes: Last night Aug 26. EUR 296.10

Name: Basic-Fit Dupleix
Notes: Self-serve kiosk, contactless card. No re-entry. Bring water.

Name: Neoness La Motte-Picquet
Notes: Best value/quality ratio in the 15th. Good AC. English-speaking staff.

Name: Fitness Park Rue du Theatre
Notes: Strong ratings. Will let you visit before signing up.

Name: Front De Seine
Notes: Reformer + RPM + boxing + sauna + squash. Best all-in-one.

Name: Deeply Studio de Pilates
Notes: Top-rated reformer in the 15th. Max 6 per class. Bilingual. Book ahead.

Name: The New Me Reformer Pilates
Notes: Clean studio. Good atmosphere. English-friendly staff.

Name: Studio15 Pilates
Notes: Air-conditioned. Reformer + exo chair + tower — rare combo in Paris. Bilingual.

Name: Club Pilates Paris Convention
Notes: High-quality equipment. Name on chalkboard on arrival.

Name: Aja House
Notes: TOP PICK for Jess. Closest to Equinox-level in Paris. Reformer + yoga + barre + cardio.

Name: Dream Seven Paris
Notes: Stunning Haussmann-style space. Instructors from dance/modeling backgrounds.

Name: Ora Studio
Notes: Boutique hotel-level design. Pilates + yoga + matcha bar. Most polished experience.

Name: Ksport Studio 7
Notes: Well-equipped. Diverse class offering. Strong community.

Name: Marche Grenelle
Address: Boulevard de Grenelle, between Dupleix and La Motte-Picquet, 15e
Notes: Home market. Produce, cheese, rotisserie chicken, flowers. Best before 11am. Dupleix end best.

Name: Marche President Wilson
Notes: Upscale market. Great cheese, charcuterie, prepared foods. More expensive but higher quality.

Name: Marche Saxe-Breteuil
Notes: Photogenic market with Eiffel Tower backdrop. Good produce, flowers, crepe stand.

Name: Marche Raspail
Notes: Sunday organic market is famous. Worth the trip once. Pricey but excellent quality.

Name: Marche d'Aligre
Notes: Most local/authentic Paris market. Cheapest produce. Great North African food stalls.

Name: Champ de Mars
Notes: Right near the apartment. Open space for Sloane to run.

Name: Jardin du Luxembourg
Notes: Puppet shows, toy sailboats, playground (paid, ~EUR 2).

Name: Tuileries Garden
Notes: Carousel, trampolines in summer.

Name: Bois de Boulogne
Notes: Rowboats, Jardin d'Acclimatation (amusement park + zoo).

Name: Eiffel Tower
Address: Champ de Mars, 5 Avenue Anatole France, 7e Paris
Notes: Advance booking required. Kid lines available. Stroller-friendly.

Name: Louvre Museum
Notes: Book timed entry. Kid-friendly wings (Egyptian, sculpture garden). Free under 18.

Name: Musee d'Orsay
Address: 1 Rue de la Legion d'Honneur, 7e Paris
Notes: Impressionists. Book ahead. Manageable size with toddler.

Name: Musee de l'Orangerie
Notes: Monet water lilies. Small and toddler-friendly (quick visit).

Name: Cite des Sciences
Notes: Interactive kids section (Cite des Enfants for ages 2-7).

Name: Philharmonie des Enfants
Notes: On itinerary Jul 28 with Anna, Ant, Roman.

Name: Jardin d'Acclimatation
Notes: Amusement park + zoo in Bois de Boulogne. Great for Sloane.

Name: Paris Plages
Address: Along the Seine, Right Bank & Bassin de la Villette
Notes: Temporary beaches mid-July to mid-August. Free. Kid-friendly zones with sprinklers.

Name: Canal Saint-Martin
Notes: Walk along the locks, cafe-lined.

Name: Versailles
Notes: Book ahead. Gardens free except fountain show days.

Name: Giverny (Monet's Garden)
Notes: Best Apr-Oct. Monet's famous gardens and water lilies.

Name: Fontainebleau
Notes: Less crowded than Versailles. Forest walks.

Name: Chantilly
Notes: Castle + horse museum + cream.

Name: Boulangerie Maison Pichard
Notes: Pain au chocolat, tartlets. Good for gouter (4pm snack).

Name: La Fontaine Gaillon
Notes: From the trip sheet.

Name: Le Bons George
Notes: Reservation with Anna, Jul 26 @ 7pm.

Name: Gordes
Notes: Hilltop village. One of the most beautiful in Luberon.

Name: Roussillon (Ochre Trail)
Notes: Famous ochre cliffs trail. Stunning red/orange landscape.

Name: Saint-Remy-de-Provence
Notes: Market days, Van Gogh trail, Roman ruins (Glanum).

Name: Bonnieux
Notes: Charming Luberon village.

Name: Menerbes
Notes: Luberon village. Peter Mayle's A Year in Provence.

Name: Mam From Hanoi
Notes: Vietnamese restaurant specializing in Northern pho. Michelin-recognized. Make reservations ASAP. Via @foodfindsryan

Name: Brasserie Martin
Notes: Classic Parisian brasserie in the 11e. Featured in best restaurants in Paris list. Via @maddiemclark

Name: Early June
Notes: Wine bar near Canal St Martin. From Dua Lipa's Paris guide. Via @mattconcierge

Name: The Hood
Notes: Creative cafe-restaurant with modern Singaporean flavours. Part of Paris's rising Asian dining scene. Via @roadbook

Name: Cafe 1902 at Petit Palais
Notes: Stunning cafe inside Petit Palais museum. Garden courtyard with fountain. Serene oasis near Champs-Elysees. Via @pariscafeproject

Name: Bistrot Paul Bert
Notes: Classic bistrot, famous for steak au poivre. Top 10 bites in Paris. Via @eat_this_

Name: Riverose Paris
Notes: Brunch spot right in front of the Eiffel Tower. Ultra-indulgent dishes and colorful drinks. Via @riveroseparis

Name: Fabula
Notes: Cafe-restaurant near Le Marais. Via @mia.in.france

Name: Onyx Restaurant Paris
Notes: Elevated lunch menu. Fine dining in the 9e. Via @seb_hed

Name: Sugaar
Notes: Basque steakhouse in Saint-Germain. Recommended by Dua Lipa in Tatler Travel guide. Via @tatlertravel

Name: Cafe-Restaurant Le Jardin du Petit Palais
Notes: Garden courtyard cafe inside Petit Palais. Fountain and museum views. A hidden quiet spot. Via @ang.and.chris

Name: Rotisserie Segar
Notes: Famous rotisserie chicken caesar sandwich on Rue Mouffetard. Via @jacksdiningroom

Name: RORI
Notes: Kid-friendly pizza spot. The Red Pie is a favorite. Great for a quick pizza break with Sloane. Via @fanny__petit

Name: Verlet Paris
Notes: Historic coffee house near Palais Royal. One of Paris's treasure spots. Via @studiocartashop

Name: La Grande Epicerie de Paris
Notes: Legendary gourmet food hall at Le Bon Marche. Over 100 years old. Clean ingredients, whole foods, artisanal products. Via @thehapawellness

Name: Marche des Enfants Rouges
Notes: Paris's oldest food market, 400 years old. Amazing variety of prepared food stalls. Via @eatingwithtod

Name: Maille Paris
Notes: Iconic mustard and condiment boutique on Place de la Madeleine. Great souvenir spot. Via @baochitravel

Name: Musee Carnavalet
Notes: Free Paris history museum in Le Marais. Beautiful courtyard. Via @marigladkaya

Name: Musee Rodin
Notes: Sunlit rooms and romantic sculpture gardens. One of the most magical spots in Paris. Via @rileejsmith

Name: Dior Museum
Notes: La Galerie Dior. EUR 16 entry. Great for a bestie day out. Via @toniscaglione

Name: Parc Monceau
Notes: Romantic, hidden-garden feel. Less structured than other Paris parks. Fantasy landscape with historic fragments. Via @toniscaglione

Name: Ile de Reuilly
Address: Avenue des Terroirs de France, 75012 Paris
Notes: Hidden Paris gem in the 12e. A side of Paris most people never see. Via @kianaatravel

Name: Burning Bar Paris
Notes: Hot yoga and pilates studio. Luxe self-care and fitness experience. Via @maddiemclark

Name: Guignol Puppet Theatre
Address: Jardin d'Acclimatation, Bois de Boulogne, 75016 Paris
Notes: Iconic French puppet theatre, 200+ years old. Beloved way to introduce kids to theatre. Great for Sloane. Via @americanmominparis

Name: Saint-Eustache Church
Notes: Immersive light experience inside the church. Free. Near Les Halles. Via @dayoneinparis

Name: Bonhomme de Bois
Notes: Classic Parisian toy shop. Great for kids. Via @littlegeneralkids

Name: Airelles Gordes, La Bastide
Notes: Luxury hotel restaurant above Gordes village. Featured in Emily in Paris. 2.5 hrs from Paris by train. Via @theluxurywifestyle
